questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the circle which passes through the points $(1,0),(0,-6)$ and $(3,4)$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{A}=(1,0), \mathrm{B}=(0,-6), \mathrm{C}=(3,4)$
Equation of $\mathrm{AB}$ is $\mathrm{L}: \frac{\mathrm{y}-0}{-6-0}=\frac{\mathrm{x}-1}{0-1}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{y}{-6}=\frac{x-1}{-1} \Rightarrow y=6 x-6$
$\Rightarrow 6 x-y-6=0 .$
Equation of circle (c) with $\mathrm{AB}$ as diameter is $(\mathrm{x}-1)(\mathrm{x}-$ $0)+(y-0)(y+6)=0$
$\Rightarrow x^{2}-x+y^{2}+6 y=0 .$
The system of circle passing through the intersection of the circle $\mathrm{C}$ and the line $\mathrm{L}$ is given by $\mathrm{C}+\mathrm{kL}=0$ $\Rightarrow x^{2}-x+y^{2}+6 y+k(6 x-y-6)=0$
This circle is passing through $(3,4)$. $\therefore(3)^{2}-3+(4)^{2}+6(4)+\mathrm{k}[6(3)-4-6]=0$
$\Rightarrow 9-3+16+24+\mathrm{k}(18-10)=0$
$\Rightarrow 46+8 \mathrm{k}=0 \Rightarrow 8 \mathrm{k}=-46 \Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=\frac{-46}{8}=\frac{-23}{4}$
$\therefore$ Equation of circle is
$x^{2}-x+y^{2}+6 y+\left(\frac{-23}{4}\right)(6 x-y-6)=0$
$\Rightarrow 4 x^{2}-4 x+4 y^{2}+24 y-138 x+23 y+138=0$
$\Rightarrow 4 x^{2}+4 y^{2}-142 x+47 y+138=0 .$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
A line is drawn through a fixed point $P(\alpha, \beta)$ to cut the circle $x^{2}+y^{2}=r^{2}$ at $A$ and $B$. Then
$P A \cdot P B$ is equal to
questions.solution:
The equation of any line through the point $P(\alpha, \beta)$ is
$$
\frac{x-\alpha}{\cos \theta}=\frac{y-\beta}{\sin \theta}=k \text { (say) }
$$
Any point on this line is
$$
(\alpha+k \cos \theta, \beta+k \sin \theta)
$$
This point lies on the given circle, if
$$
(\alpha+k \cos \theta)^{2}+(\beta+k \sin \theta)^{2}=r^{2}
$$
or $k^{2}+2 k(\alpha \cos \theta+\beta \sin \theta)$
$$
+\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}-r^{2}=0
$$
Which being quadratic in $k$, gives two values of $k$. Let $P A=k_{1}, P B=k_{2}$, where $k_{1}, k_{2}$ are the roots of Eq. (i), then
$$
P A \cdot P B=k_{1} k_{2}=\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}-r^{2}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the coordinates at one end of a diameter of the circle $x^{2}+y^{2}-8 x-4 y+c=0$ are $(-3,2)$, then the coordinates at the other end are
questions.solution:
The centre of the given circle is $\mathrm{C} \equiv(4,2)$ Let $\mathrm{A} \equiv(-3,2)$


If $(\alpha, \beta)$ are the coordinates of the other end of the diameter, then, as the middle ploint of the diameter is the centre,
$\therefore \quad \frac{\alpha-3}{2}=4$ and $\frac{\beta+2}{2}=2 \Rightarrow \alpha=11, \beta=2$
Thus, the coordinates of the other end of diameter are $(11,2)$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Point $(1,2)$ relative to the circle $x^{2}+y^{2}+4 x-2 y-4=0$ is a/ an
questions.solution:
We put the co-ordinates of the given point in the given equation of circle $x^{2}+y^{2}+4 x-2 y-4=0$
$\operatorname{At}(1,2)$
$(1)^{2}+(2)^{2}+4(1)-2(2)-4$
$=1+4+4-4-4=1>0$
$\Rightarrow$ Point $(1,2)$ lies out side the circle i.e, an exterior point.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Area of the circle in which a chord of length $\sqrt{2}$ makes an angle $\pi / 2$ at the centre, is
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{AB}$ be the chord of length $\sqrt{2}$. Let $\mathrm{O}$ be the centre of the circle and let $O C$ be the perpendicular from $\mathrm{O}$ on $\mathrm{AB}$.
Then $, \mathrm{AC}=\mathrm{BC}=\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
In $\Delta \mathrm{OBC},$ we have
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{OB} &=\mathrm{BC} \operatorname{cosec} 45^{\circ} \\
&=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \times \sqrt{2}=1
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Area of the circle $=\pi(\mathrm{OB})^{2}=\pi$ sq units
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the line $x \cos \alpha+y \sin \alpha=p$ represents the common chord of the circles $x^{2}+y^{2}=a^{2}$ and $\mathrm{x}^{2}+\mathrm{y}^{2}+\mathrm{b}^{2}(\mathrm{a}>\mathrm{b}),$ where $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ lie ont he first circle and $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{Q}$ lie on the second circle, then AP is equal to
questions.solution: The given circles are concentric with centre at (0,0) and the length of the perpendicular from (0,0) on the given line is $\mathrm{p}$. Let $\mathrm{OL}=\mathrm{p}$ then, $\mathrm{AL}=\sqrt{\mathrm{OA}^{2}-\mathrm{OL}^{2}}=\sqrt{\mathrm{a}^{2}-\mathrm{p}^{2}}$ and $\mathrm{PL}=\sqrt{\mathrm{OP}^{2}-\mathrm{OL}^{2}}=\sqrt{\mathrm{b}^{2}-\mathrm{p}^{2}}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{AP}=\sqrt{\mathrm{a}^{2}-\mathrm{p}^{2}}-\sqrt{\mathrm{b}^{2}-\mathrm{p}^{2}}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
If the extremities of a diameter of a circle are $(0,0)$ and
$\left(\mathrm{a}^{3}, 1 / \mathrm{a}^{3}\right)$, then the circle passes through which one of the
following points?
questions.solution:
Given the extremities of a diameter of a circle as
$(0,0)$ and $\left(\mathrm{a}^{3}, \frac{1}{\mathrm{a}}\right)$, equation of circle is
$(x-0)\left(x-a^{3}\right)+(y-0)\left(y-\frac{1}{a^{3}}\right)=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad x^{2}-x a^{3}+y^{2}-\frac{y}{a^{3}}=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad x^{2}+y^{2}-x a^{3}-\frac{y}{a^{3}}=0$
Putting $\mathrm{x}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}}$ and $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{a}$, the equation is satisfied.
Thus, the circle passes through the point $\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{a}}, \mathrm{a}\right)$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If one of the diameter of the circle, given by the equation $x^{2}+y^{2}+4 x+6 y-12=0,$ is a chord of a circle $S,$ whose centre is $(2,-3$ ), the radius of $S$ is
questions.solution: Given, equation of circle is $x^{2}+y^{2}+4 x+6 y-12=0$ Centre $C(-2,-3)$ and radius $=\sqrt{(-2)^{2}+(-3)^{2}+12}=5$ $$ x^{2}+y^{2}+4 x+6 y-12=0 $$ $$ \begin{aligned} C_{1} C_{2} &=\sqrt{\left(2+{2})^{2}+(-3+3)^{2}\right.} \\ &=\sqrt{(4)^{2}+(0)^{2}}=4 \end{aligned} $$ $\therefore$ Radius of circle, $S$ $$ =\sqrt{(4)^{2}+({5})^{2}}=\sqrt{16+25}=\sqrt{41} \text { unit } $$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $(3, \lambda \lambda \lambda \lambda)$ and $(5,6)$ are the conjugate points to the curve $x^{2}+y^{2}=3$, then $\lambda \lambda \lambda \lambda$ is
questions.solution:
If the $(3, \lambda \lambda \lambda \lambda)$ and $(5,6)$ are the conjugate points to the curve $x^{2}+y^{2}=3$, then
$\begin{aligned} x_{1} x_{2}+y_{1} y_{2} &=3 \\ \Rightarrow \quad(3)(5)+(\lambda \lambda \lambda \lambda) \cdot(6) &=3 \end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 15+6(\lambda \lambda \lambda \lambda)=3$
$\Rightarrow 6(\lambda \lambda \lambda \lambda)=-12$
$\Rightarrow \lambda \lambda \lambda \lambda=-2$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A circle of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> units is touching both the axes and a circle with centre at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mo>)</mo></math>. The distance between their centers is
questions.solution:
Radius of smaller circle is r=2 units (given)

Since, it is touching both the axes, so its centre is (2, 2)



The distance between their centers

=r+R= 5-22+6-22

= 32+42= 25=5 units

questions.q: If the circles described on the line joining the points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> as diameter cuts the axis of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in points whose abscissae are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi> </mi><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Equation of circle in diameter





x- 0x-α+ y- 1y-β=0

on x axis y=0

x2-αx +β=0 ...1 

x2- 5x+3=0 ...2 

Compare equation 1 & 2

α=5, β=3

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> is a chord of the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> subtending a right angle at the centre. Then, the locus of the centroid of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Δ</mi><mi>P</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> moves on the circle, is
questions.solution:
The given circle has the centre at the origin.

Let Prcosθ,rsinθ and assuming A&B lie on the axes then their coordinates are r,0 & 0,r.

If (h,k) is the centroid of the ΔPAB, then

h=r(1+cosθ)3

And k=r(1+sinθ)3

⇒h-r32+k-r32=r32

Hence, locus of h,k is

x-r32+y-r32=r32 which is a circle.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The line $y=m x+c$ intercepts the circle $x^2+y^2=r^2$ in two distinct points, if
questions.solution:
Equation of the circle is
$$
x^2+y^2=r^2
$$
and the line is $m x-y+c=0$
The line (ii) intersect (i) in two distinct points, if the length of the $\perp^r$ from the centre $(0,0)$ to the line (ii) is less then $r$
Thus, $\left|\frac{0-0+c}{\sqrt{m^2+1}}\right| < r$
$$
\begin{array}{ccc}
\Rightarrow & |c| < r \sqrt{m^2+1} \\
\Rightarrow & -r \sqrt{m^2+1} < c < r \sqrt{m^2+1}
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: The equation of the circle which touches the X-axis and Y-axis at the points (1, 0) and (0, 1) respectively is
questions.solution:
On plotting the diagram based on the given information, we find centre = (1,1) and radius = 1
$\therefore$ Equation of circle, $(x-1)^2+(y-1)^2=1^2$ $$ x^2+y^2-2 x-2 y+1=0 $$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The perpendicular distance from the point $(1,2)$ to common chord of the circles $x^2+y^2-2 x+4 y-4=0$ and $x^2+y^2+4 x-6 y-3=0$ is........ units.
questions.solution:
Given circle,
$x^2+y^2-2 x+4 y-4=0$
and $x^2+y^2+4 x-6 y-3=0$
Equation of common chord of circle is $S_1-S_2=0$
$\therefore \quad\left(x^2+y^2-2 x+4 y-4\right)$
$-\left(x^2+y^2+4 x-6 y-3\right)=0$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & -6 x+10 y-1=0 \\ \Rightarrow & 6 x-10 y+1=0\end{array}$
Perpendicular distance from the point $(1,2)$ to the line $6 x-10 y+1=0$ is $\left|\frac{6-20+1}{\sqrt{6^2+10^2}}\right|$
$=\frac{13}{\sqrt{136}}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: From the point  A0,3  on the circle x2+4x+y−32=0 , a chord  ABis drawn and extended to a point M such that  AM=2AB . The equation of the locus of M is:
questions.solution:
The equation of circle is,

 x2+y2+4x−6y+9=0...1

Given,

 AM=2AB

⇒AB=MB

Let the co-ordinates of M be  h,k

Then, B is mid point of AM and its coordinates are –

 B0+h2,3+k2or Bh2,k+32

Now put B in eq. of circle

To get h24+2h+k+32-32=0

⇒h2+8h+k-32=0

⇒h2+k2+8h-6k+9=0

PQ2=(OP-OQ)2PQ2=x02+x02-12  [∵0Q=1]

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let $S$ be the circle in $x y$-plane which touches the $x$-axis at point $A$, the $y$-axis at point $B$ and the unit circle $x^{2}+y^{2}=1$ at point $C$ externally. If $O$ denotes the origin, then the angle OCA equals
questions.solution: $\therefore$ Slope of $O M=1$ $\therefore \angle \mathrm{COA}=45^{\circ}$ $\mathrm{C}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}, \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)$ Now OM $=O C+C M$ $\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~h}=1+\mathrm{h}$ Squaring $\begin{array}{l} 2 h^{2}=h^{2}+1+2 h \\ h^{2}-2 h-1=0 \end{array}$ $\mathrm{h}=\frac{2 \pm \sqrt{8}}{2} \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}=1 \pm \sqrt{2}$ $\mathrm{~A}(1+\sqrt{2}, 0)$ Slope of $A C=\frac{0-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{1+\sqrt{2}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}+1}=-(\sqrt{2}-1)$ $=-\tan 22 \frac{1}{2}^{\circ}=\tan \left(180-22 \frac{1}{2}\right)^{\circ}=\tan 157 \frac{1}{2}^{\circ}$ $\therefore \angle \mathrm{CAX}=157 \frac{1}{2}^{\circ}$ $\therefore \angle \mathrm{OCA}=157 \frac{1}{2}^{\circ}-45^{\circ}=112 \frac{1}{2}^{\circ}=\frac{5 \pi}{8}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For how many values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math>, the circle <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>p</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and the coordinate axes have exactly three common points?
questions.solution:
We shall consider 3 cases.

Case I: When p = 0

(i.e., circle passes through origin)

Now, equation of circle becomes

x2+y2+2x+4y=0

Case II: When circle intersect x-axis at 2 distinct points and touches y-axis.

g 2 −c>0 and f 2 = c

1+p>0 and p=-4 so, this case is not possible

Case III: When circle intersect y-axis at 2 distinct points and touches x-axis

Now, g2-c=0  and f2-c>0

⇒1--p=0 and 4--p>0

⇒    p=-1  and, p>-4

∴   p=-1 is possible.

∴   Finally, we conclude that p=0,-1.

⇒Two possible values of p.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
